## [00:00 - 03:03]

All right, so at this point you have likely been through the whole Kajabi course for the recruiting coordinator position and gone through all of the training. So this is just going to be a recap about partly what you've kind of already done, but what is going to happen from here moving forward. So in terms of expectations, every single day we have a recruiting meeting at 10 a.m. So this is with Ry, Jacob, and myself. Now on Monday and Wednesday we have a team meeting at 12 p.m. Pacific time. Not a whole lot of this meeting is going to require your active participation. However, you're going to learn a lot on these meetings. It's really good for team culture and kind of overall mood throughout your week. Everyone on our team really, really enjoys the team meetings. So those will be the meetings. Sometimes we'll have, you know, random meetings that we'll add in, but for the most part they're pretty consistent at those times. Now day one and day two when you initially come on, you've likely already gone through and set up everything that you need with the platform, gone through the necessary documents, agreements, and things like that. If not, just let me know and we can get those over to you. Once you've completed all of the agreements and you've gone up on the platform, you should have already gone through the whole Kajabi portal and the training program and written down any notes, any questions that you have. You also have likely completed the homework. Now if you have already watched that, I would recommend going back and re-watching those videos another time around. Watch them at least two times each the second time. You can play them at 1.5, the speed just goes through them a little bit quicker, but that would be the best way to ramp up on these interviews is by really watching them and getting to understand the lingo and the questions that we ask and red flags that we often see. Then the last thing that is needed is just to watch the mission and the values video, which is also located in Kajabi. Now once you've been through the whole training platform and everything's set up in Rocking and Rollin and you are ready to get going on interviews, what's going to happen on day three and four is you're going to shadow your ride on all of his interviews because he's involved in some other things with us. He's not doing 15 interviews a day. He's usually doing 10 to 12. So you'll shadow him on 10 to 12 interviews each day. You'll complete your own notes as you listen in just to kind of get in the rhythm of taking notes while listening and we'll give you feedback in your end of day because you'll put them in your end of day. We'll give you feedback on anything that might need to be changed. At the end of day four, that's kind of the second day of actually being on interviews, we will do a brief call at the end of the day with Jigba and myself just to kind of see how you're feeling, any questions you have and to kind of realign for going to day five and six. Day five and six, you'll have your ride on the interviews. We'll be booked on his calendar but he'll add you into them and you'll conduct the entire

interview and then your ride can give you coaching at the end and really pay attention to anything that you have questions about or positives, negatives, any notes coming up and you can put those in your end of day as long or as well with the notes that you took on those interviews.
